validation_meta.method: Compact event windows cross-checked against public web sources and the macro release calendar (CPI 12:30 UTC = 08:30 ET; FOMC 18:00 UTC = 14:00 ET, presser 18:30 UTC). No trading advice. 'No Clear Cause' preferred over forced explanation.

baseline.verdict: Windows are too long and direction-agnostic. 12 multi-hour 'market_day two_sided' blobs (up to 21h15m) cannot map to a single catalyst and bury the real CPI/FOMC reaction windows inside all-day envelopes. The detector does catch the genuine macro days (Jun 10 CPI, Jun 17 FOMC, Jun 18 post-FOMC) but only as vague long windows.

vnext_a.verdict: Compact, directionally-clean windows. The two scheduled macro catalysts land in exact 15-min windows (CPI 12:30 UTC, FOMC 18:00/19:15 UTC), and the FOMC whipsaw is correctly split into down/up/down sub-events instead of one 'two_sided' blob. BUT source_likelihood_score is miscalibrated: it scores ALL 25 events 'high' (>=0.7) while only 7 are actually source-explainable. ~13 events are weekend/overnight micro-moves (<1.6%, several <1.0%) with no findable catalyst.

validation_summary.reason: vNext-A wins on windowing and usefulness: every source-supported catalyst maps to a tight, direction-correct window, and it eliminates all 12 multi-hour baseline blobs that are inherently unexplainable. Its remaining flaw is over-firing and over-scoring low-magnitude weekend/overnight moves, which is fixable with a magnitude gate and by renaming/decoupling the source-likelihood score from breadth/strength. Baseline's all-day two_sided envelopes are not source-explainable by construction.

events.reason: 1.27% breadth-5 up move on a Thursday afternoon with no scheduled release and no findable catalyst. Compact but small; no public source ties anything to this 15-min window.
Only 0.96% max window move. Below a meaningful market-event magnitude; nothing to explain.

1.98% breadth-5 down, but Saturday 07:45 UTC (low-liquidity, pre-US). Sits in the late-May volatile run-up to the early-June selloff but no source explains this specific window. Detector strength=100 yet source support is low — a clear score/source mismatch.

Backdrop, not a focused catalyst.

0.97% move, overnight (02:00 UTC), event_strength only 41.7. Below useful magnitude.
1.05% up (SOL-led). Solana spot ETFs began trading May 26, but US ETF trading starts at 13:30 UTC (09:30 ET); a 10:15 UTC move is too early to be the launch. No catalyst aligns.
1.46% breadth-5 down (BTC-led) during US hours. Consistent with the building late-May risk-off / ETF-outflow backdrop but no source names this specific window.

1.60% up, event_strength only 44.9, no catalyst found around this window.

2.03% breadth-5 down (XRP-led) inside the early-June crash: US-Iran escalation (Strait of Hormuz threat, oil spike), Strategy's first BTC sale since 2022 (disclosed Jun 1), and a record multi-day ETF outflow streak. Strong backdrop, but no source pins this exact 14:15 UTC window — it is one drop within a multi-day risk-off slide.

events.reason: Largest move in the set: 4.13% breadth-5 up (SOL-led), score 0.99. Public recaps describe a ~4% Sunday Jun 7 relief rally after Michael Saylor/Strategy signaled resumption of BTC accumulation (having broken the 'never sell' rule the prior week). Magnitude matches the reported '4% rally'; direction and day align. Timing to the exact 22:00 UTC window is approximate, so Likely rather than Focused.

sources.why_relevant: Reports the ~4% Jun 7 rebound above $63K attributed to Saylor/Strategy re-accumulation signal — matches this event's size and direction.

events.reason: 1.59% breadth-5 down in the very next 15-min bar after the Jun 7 spike — a mechanical retrace of the same rally, not a separate catalyst. Detector opened a new opposite-direction event on the immediate pullback. Candidate for merging with the prior up-event rather than surfacing as its own.

Exact alignment with the May-CPI release at 08:30 ET = 12:30 UTC on Jun 10. CPI printed a hot headline (4.2% YoY, hottest since 2023) but a SOFT core (2.9% YoY, 0.2% MoM vs 0.3% est); the soft core drove a modest risk-positive pop. The detector's window opens on the exact release bar — textbook scheduled-catalyst alignment.

IMPORTANT CALIBRATION CASE: the detector's TOP-scored event (score 1.0, strength 100, 3.23% SOL-led) — yet it is Sunday 21:15 UTC with NO findable catalyst. High detector confidence with zero source support proves source_likelihood_score is measuring signal magnitude, not source availability. A real 3% breadth-5 weekend move, but unexplained — show only with a 'no public catalyst found' label, never as 'high source likelihood'.

events.reason: 1.32% up (XRP-led), Sunday pre-US 11:15 UTC. Altcoin-ETF inflows were reported around Jun 15 but those are US-hours flows; no source aligns to this window.

Exact alignment with the FOMC decision at 14:00 ET = 18:00 UTC on Jun 17. The Fed held at 3.50-3.75% (4th hold) but delivered a HAWKISH dot plot (9/18 members projecting >=1 hike, 6 projecting two) and raised the PCE forecast to 3.6% — a risk-off shock under new Chair Warsh. The window opens on the exact statement bar with a down move.

events.reason: Worst structural failure: a 21h15m 'two_sided' envelope covering all of Jun 7. Even though Jun 7 contained the real ~4% Saylor relief rally (~22:00 UTC), this all-day blob cannot be tied to any single catalyst and hides the actual move. vNext-A correctly isolated the 22:00 spike. Not publishable as-is.

events.reason: Baseline DOES catch the CPI reaction and opens on the exact 12:30 UTC bar — good. But it merges the release pop and the US-open continuation into one 75-min window. vNext-A's split (12:30 Focused + 13:30 Likely) is cleaner. Same catalyst, coarser window.

sources.title: Bitcoin CPI Reaction: BTC Holds $61K After Mixed Inflation Report
sources.why_relevant: Confirms the Jun 10 CPI catalyst for this window.

events.reason: Baseline catches the FOMC session but collapses the entire down/up/down whipsaw into ONE 'two_sided' 90-min blob, erasing the directional information a reader needs. vNext-A's three directional sub-events (18:00 down / 18:30 up / 19:15 down) are far more useful and source-explainable. This single comparison is the strongest argument for vNext-A.

events.reason: 8h45m two_sided envelope over the early-June selloff. The backdrop (Iran/ETF outflows/Strategy sale) is real but an all-day two_sided window is not a publishable 'event'. vNext-A isolated two compact drops (14:15, 22:45) within this same day.

events.event_id: baseline market_day blobs (10 others: 05-21, 05-23, 05-24, 05-26, 05-27, 05-29, 06-04, 06-05, 06-15 + short overnight market_wide)
events.reason: Aggregate entry for the remaining baseline events: ~9 more multi-hour 'market_day two_sided' envelopes (255-1080 min) that are direction-agnostic and unmappable to catalysts, plus several 15-min overnight market_wide blips (e.g. 06-06 05:15, 06-09 04:30, 06-10 00:45) with no findable cause. None are publishable as discrete, source-explainable events.

threshold_feedback: triggerMovePctMin (0.35%) and sustainMovePctMin (0.20%) are far too low for a PUBLIC event: they admit 0.86-1.0% 'events' (May 22, May 25, Jun 14 14:30) that are noise. Add a publish gate on max_abs_window_move_pct (suggest >= ~1.5%, tune on more history) separate from the trigger logic so detection stays sensitive but display stays meaningful.

All 25 vNext-A events were breadth 5/5, so breadthMin=3 never bound in practice — breadth is not the discriminator here; magnitude and source-context are. Consider requiring stronger confirmation (volume_ratio) for sub-1.5% moves before publishing.

Baseline: cap event duration. It produced 12 events >2h (up to 1275 min). Port vNext-A's maxDurationBars/calm-bar close to the baseline, or retire baseline's market_day two_sided concept for public output entirely.
window_feedback: vNext-A windowing is the win: compact, direction-clean, and it correctly SPLIT the Jun 17 FOMC whipsaw (down/up/down) that baseline buried in one 90-min two_sided blob.

Add micro-retrace merging: Jun 7 22:00 up (+4.13%) immediately followed by 22:15 down (-1.59%) are the same catalyst (spike + mechanical retrace). Suppress or merge an opposite-direction event that opens within 1 bar and is smaller than the parent.

Consider snapping/annotating windows to the macro calendar: when a window opens within +/-1 bar of a scheduled release (CPI 12:30 UTC, FOMC 18:00/18:30 UTC, NFP, PCE), tag it 'macro-aligned' — this is exactly where source support was highest.
evidence_model_feedback: CRITICAL: source_likelihood_score is mislabeled. It is computed purely from breadth/strength/magnitude/volume and has ZERO knowledge of whether a public source exists. Result: all 25 events scored 'high' (>=0.7) but only 7 are source-explainable, and the single highest-scored event (Jun 14 21:15, score 1.0) has NO catalyst. Rename to 'signal_strength_score' and stop presenting it as source likelihood.

Introduce a true source-context signal that is independent of price: (a) macro-calendar proximity flag, (b) time-of-day/day-of-week liquidity flag (overnight 00:00-06:00 UTC and weekends correlated strongly with 'No Clear Cause' here). 8 of 10 No-Clear-Cause events were weekend or overnight.
Add an explicit 'No Clear Cause' as a first-class, preferred outcome in the evidence model so the pipeline does not force a backdrop narrative onto unexplained weekend/overnight moves.
claude_payload_feedback: Include both UTC and ET timestamps and day-of-week in the payload — the CPI/FOMC alignment is only visible once you convert to ET (12:30 UTC = 08:30 ET; 18:00 UTC = 14:00 ET).
Include a macro-calendar context block (nearest scheduled US release within the window and its delta in minutes) so Claude can distinguish a scheduled-catalyst event from a weekend micro-move.

Instruct Claude to return 'No Clear Cause' when the window is overnight/weekend AND no dated, time-aligned source exists — and to never upgrade generic market commentary or price-recap articles to a cause (keep the existing no_trading_advice flag).
Pass per-symbol magnitude and the lead_mover so Claude can sanity-check coin-specific vs market-wide narratives (e.g. an ETH-led move undercuts a SOL-specific story).
ui_label_feedback: Stop showing 'Source likelihood: high' derived from the current score — it was 'high' for events with no source. Replace with a verified label set: 'Catalyst confirmed' (Focused), 'Likely cause', 'Backdrop only', 'No clear cause'.
Add a 'Macro-aligned (CPI/FOMC)' badge for windows matching the release calendar — these are the events most worth surfacing.
Visually de-emphasize or collapse weekend/overnight sub-1.5% moves; if shown, label 'low-liquidity, no public catalyst found' rather than implying explainability.
Never render baseline 'market_day two_sided' multi-hour envelopes as public events; they read as 'something happened today' and erode trust.
